server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c - Server-side handshake protocol implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport)
Server: Send CRYPTO_PARAMETERS and set handshake context sizes. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_start (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport)
Server: Start crypto handshake by sending public key. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Server: Process client's public key and send auth challenge. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_complete (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Server: Process auth response and complete handshake.

Detailed Description

Server-side handshake protocol implementation.

Definition in file lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t payload_len)

Server: Process client's public key and send auth challenge.

Parameters

ctx Handshake context (must be in CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE state)
transport ACIP transport to send on
packet_type Packet type received (should be PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP or PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION)
payload Received packet payload
payload_len Length of received payload

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Server processes client's KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP packet and sends AUTH_CHALLENGE. Computes shared secret and generates authentication challenge nonce.

Note

Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling this function

State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE -> CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING

Definition at line 164 of file lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.

166                                                                              {
167   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE) {
168     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
169   }
170   if (!transport) {
171     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
172   }
173 
174   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
175   int result;
176 
177   // Check if client sent NO_ENCRYPTION response
178   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION) {
179 
180     // Send AUTH_FAILED to inform client (though they already know)
181     auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
182     failure.reason_flags = 0; // No specific auth failure, just encryption mismatch
183     int send_result =
184         packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
185     if (send_result != 0) {
186       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send AUTH_FAILED packet");
187     }
188 
189     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "SECURITY: Client sent NO_ENCRYPTION response - encryption mode "
190                                    "mismatch. Server requires encryption, but "
191                                    "client has --no-encrypt. Use matching encryption settings on "
192                                    "both client and server");
193   }
194 
195   // Verify packet type
196   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP) {
197     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE, got packet type %d", packet_type);
198   }
199 
200   // Verify payload size - client can send either simple or authenticated format
201   // Simple: kex_public_key_size bytes
202   // Authenticated: public_key_size + client_auth_key_size + client_sig_size bytes
203   size_t simple_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size;
204   // Ed25519 public key is always 32 bytes
205   // Ed25519 signature is always 64 bytes
206   size_t authenticated_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE + ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;
207 
208   bool client_sent_identity = false;
209   uint8_t *client_ephemeral_key = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, uint8_t *);
210   uint8_t *client_identity_key = SAFE_MALLOC(ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, uint8_t *);
211   uint8_t *client_signature = SAFE_MALLOC(ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE, uint8_t *);
212 
213   if (!client_ephemeral_key || !client_identity_key || !client_signature) {
214     if (client_ephemeral_key)
215       SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
216     if (client_identity_key)
217       SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
218     if (client_signature)
219       SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
220     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for client keys");
221   }
222 
223   // Validate packet size using session parameters
224   asciichat_error_t validation_result =
225       crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, payload_len);
226   if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
227     // Payload ownership remains with caller
228     SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
229     SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
230     SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
231     return validation_result;
232   }
233 
234   // Handle both authenticated and non-authenticated responses
235   // authenticated_size is now a minimum - packet may be larger with GPG key ID
236   if (payload_len >= authenticated_size) {
237     // Authenticated format (may have optional GPG key ID):
238     // [ephemeral:kex_size][identity:auth_size][signature:sig_size][gpg_key_id_len:1][gpg_key_id:0-16]
239     memcpy(client_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
240     memcpy(client_identity_key, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
241     memcpy(client_signature, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,
242            ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE);
243     client_sent_identity = true;
244     ctx->client_sent_identity = true;
245 
246     // Extract GPG key ID if present
247     const char *client_gpg_key_id = NULL;
248     char gpg_key_id_buffer[41] = {0};
249     size_t gpg_offset = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE + ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;
250     if (payload_len > gpg_offset) {
251       uint8_t gpg_key_id_len = payload[gpg_offset];
252       if (gpg_key_id_len > 0 && gpg_key_id_len <= 40 && payload_len >= gpg_offset + 1 + gpg_key_id_len) {
253         memcpy(gpg_key_id_buffer, payload + gpg_offset + 1, gpg_key_id_len);
254         gpg_key_id_buffer[gpg_key_id_len] = '\0';
255         client_gpg_key_id = gpg_key_id_buffer;
256         log_debug("Extracted client GPG key ID from KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE: %s", client_gpg_key_id);
257       }
258     }
259 
260     // Check if client sent a null identity key (all zeros)
261     bool client_has_null_identity = true;
262     for (size_t i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
263       if (client_identity_key[i] != 0) {
264         client_has_null_identity = false;
265         break;
266       }
267     }
268 
269     if (client_has_null_identity) {
270       // Client has no identity key - this is allowed for servers without client authentication
271       log_debug("Client sent null identity key - no client authentication required");
272       client_sent_identity = false;
273       ctx->client_sent_identity = false;
274       log_warn("Client connected without identity authentication");
275     } else {
276       // Client has a real identity key
277       // If server didn't specify --client-keys, skip signature verification
278       // (server doesn't care about client identity verification)
279       if (!ctx->require_client_auth) {
280         log_info("Skipping client signature verification (no --client-keys specified)");
281         log_warn("Connection is encrypted but client identity is NOT verified");
282       } else {
283         // Verify client's signature
284         log_debug("Verifying client's signature");
285         // Pass client's GPG key ID if available
286         if (ed25519_verify_signature(client_identity_key, client_ephemeral_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
287                                      client_signature, client_gpg_key_id) != 0) {
288           // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
289           auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
290           failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID;
291           int send_result =
292               packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
293           if (send_result != 0) {
294             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send AUTH_FAILED packet");
295           }
296 
297           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Client signature verification FAILED - rejecting connection");
298         }
299       }
300     }
301 
302     // Store the verified client identity for whitelist checking (only if client has identity)
303     if (client_sent_identity) {
304       ctx->client_ed25519_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
305       memcpy(ctx->client_ed25519_key.key, client_identity_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
306     }
307   } else if (payload_len == simple_size) {
308     // Non-authenticated format: [ephemeral:public_key_size] only
309     log_debug("Client sent non-authenticated response (%zu bytes)", payload_len);
310     memcpy(client_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
311     client_sent_identity = false;
312     ctx->client_sent_identity = false;
313     log_warn("Client connected without identity authentication");
314   } else {
315     // Payload ownership remains with caller
316     SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
317     SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
318     SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
319     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
320                      "Invalid client key response size: %zu bytes (expected %zu for "
321                      "authenticated or %zu for simple)",
322                      payload_len, authenticated_size, simple_size);
323   }
324 
325   // Extract pointers for compatibility with existing code
326   const uint8_t *client_x25519 = client_ephemeral_key;
327   const uint8_t *client_ed25519 = client_sent_identity ? client_identity_key : NULL;
328 
329   // Check client Ed25519 key against whitelist if client provided one and
330   // whitelist is enabled
331   if (client_sent_identity && ctx->require_client_auth && ctx->client_whitelist && ctx->num_whitelisted_clients > 0) {
332     bool key_found = false;
333 
334     // Debug: print client's Ed25519 identity key
335     char client_ed25519_hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
336     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
337       safe_snprintf(client_ed25519_hex + i * 2, 3, "%02x", client_ed25519[i]);
338     }
339     log_debug("Client Ed25519 identity key: %s", client_ed25519_hex);
340 
341     // Compare against whitelist (direct Ed25519 comparison - no conversion!)
342     for (size_t i = 0; i < ctx->num_whitelisted_clients; i++) {
343       // Debug: print whitelist Ed25519 key
344       char whitelist_ed25519_hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
345       for (int j = 0; j < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; j++) {
346         safe_snprintf(whitelist_ed25519_hex + j * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->client_whitelist[i].key[j]);
347       }
348       log_debug("Whitelist[%zu] Ed25519 key: %s", i, whitelist_ed25519_hex);
349 
350       // Direct comparison of Ed25519 keys (constant-time to prevent timing
351       // attacks)
352       if (sodium_memcmp(client_ed25519, ctx->client_whitelist[i].key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) == 0) {
353         key_found = true;
354         ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified = true;
355 
356         // Store the client's Ed25519 key for signature verification
357         memcpy(&ctx->client_ed25519_key, &ctx->client_whitelist[i], sizeof(public_key_t));
358 
359         log_debug("Client Ed25519 key authorized (whitelist entry %zu)", i);
360         if (strlen(ctx->client_whitelist[i].comment) > 0) {
361           log_info("Client identity: %s", ctx->client_whitelist[i].comment);
362         }
363         break;
364       }
365     }
366 
367     if (!key_found) {
368       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Client Ed25519 key not in whitelist - rejecting connection");
369       // Don't send AUTH_FAILED here - wait until server_complete
370       // Just mark that the key was rejected
371       ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified = false;
372     }
373   } else if (client_sent_identity) {
374     // No whitelist checking - just store the client's Ed25519 key for later
375     // (Already stored at line 313-314, but keep this for clarity)
376     ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified = false;
377   }
378 
379   // Set peer's X25519 encryption key - this also derives the shared secret
380   crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_set_peer_public_key(&ctx->crypto_ctx, client_x25519);
381   if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
382     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to set peer public key and derive shared secret: %s",
383                      crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
384   }
385 
386   // Clean up allocated memory
387   SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
388   SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
389   SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
390 
391   // Do authentication challenge if client provided identity key OR server
392   // requires password
393   if (client_sent_identity || ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password || ctx->require_client_auth) {
394     // Generate nonce and store it in the context
395     crypto_result = crypto_generate_nonce(ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce);
396     if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
397       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to generate nonce: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
398     }
399 
400     // Prepare AUTH_CHALLENGE packet: 1 byte flags + auth_challenge_size byte nonce
401     size_t challenge_packet_size = AUTH_CHALLENGE_FLAGS_SIZE + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
402     uint8_t challenge_packet[1 + HMAC_SHA256_SIZE]; // Maximum size buffer
403     uint8_t auth_flags = 0;
404 
405     // Set flags based on server requirements
406     if (ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password) {
407       auth_flags |= AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD;
408     }
409     if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
410       auth_flags |= AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY;
411     }
412 
413     challenge_packet[0] = auth_flags;
414     memcpy(challenge_packet + 1, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
415 
416     // Send AUTH_CHALLENGE with flags + nonce (challenge_packet_size bytes)
417     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE, challenge_packet,
418                                        challenge_packet_size, 0);
419     if (result != 0) {
420       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send AUTH_CHALLENGE packet");
421     }
422 
423     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING;
424   } else {
425     // No authentication needed - skip to completion
426     log_debug("Skipping authentication (no password and client has no identity key)");
427 
428     // Send HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE immediately
429     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, NULL, 0, 0);
430     if (result != 0) {
431       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE packet");
432     }
433 
434     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
435     ctx->crypto_ctx.handshake_complete = true; // Mark crypto context as ready for rekeying
436     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication)");
437   }
438 
439   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
440 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, AUTH_CHALLENGE_FLAGS_SIZE,
    crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID, crypto_context_t::auth_nonce,
    AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY, AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key_verified,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_sent_identity,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_whitelist,
    public_key_t::comment, crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_generate_nonce(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), crypto_set_peer_public_key(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    ed25519_verify_signature(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, crypto_context_t::handshake_complete,
    crypto_context_t::has_password, HEX_STRING_SIZE_32,
    HMAC_SHA256_SIZE, public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    log_debug, log_info, log_warn,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::num_whitelisted_clients,
    packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size,
    auth_failure_packet_t::reason_flags,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(), SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and public_key_t::type.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    session_server_like_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_complete
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport,
  packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t
  payload_len)


Server: Process auth response and complete handshake.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING state)


transport ACIP transport to send on


packet_type Packet type received (should be
  PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE)


payload Received packet payload


payload_len Length of received payload


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Server processes client's AUTH_RESPONSE packet and sends
    SERVER_AUTH_RESP. Verifies authentication HMAC or signature and completes
    handshake.




Note


Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling
  this function



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY




Definition at line 442 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


444                                                                        {
445   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING) {
446     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
447   }
448   if (!transport) {
449     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
450   }
451 
452   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
453   int result;
454 
455   // Verify packet type
456   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE) {
457     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected AUTH_RESPONSE, got packet type %d", packet_type);
458   }
459 
460   // Verify password if required
461   if (ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password) {
462     // Validate packet size using session parameters
463     asciichat_error_t validation_result =
464         crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE, payload_len);
465     if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
466       return validation_result;
467     }
468 
469     // Ensure shared secret is derived before password verification
470     // This is critical for password HMAC verification which binds to the shared secret
471     if (!ctx->crypto_ctx.key_exchange_complete) {
472       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Password authentication failed - key exchange not complete");
473 
474       // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
475       auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
476       failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT;
477       if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
478         failure.reason_flags |= AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED;
479       }
480       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
481       return ERROR_NETWORK;
482     }
483 
484     // Verify password HMAC (binds to DH shared_secret to prevent MITM)
485     log_debug("Verifying password HMAC: has_password=%d, key_exchange_complete=%d", ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password,
486               ctx->crypto_ctx.key_exchange_complete);
487     if (!payload) {
488       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Payload is NULL in password authentication");
489       return ERROR_CRYPTO;
490     }
491     if (!crypto_verify_auth_response(&ctx->crypto_ctx, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce, payload)) {
492       log_debug("Password HMAC verification failed");
493       // Enhanced error message when both password and whitelist are required
494       if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
495         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO,
496                   "Password authentication failed - incorrect password (server also requires whitelisted client key)");
497       } else {
498         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Password authentication failed - incorrect password");
499       }
500 
501       // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
502       auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
503       failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT;
504       if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
505         failure.reason_flags |= AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED;
506       }
507       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
508       return ERROR_NETWORK;
509     }
510 
511     // Extract client challenge nonce for mutual authentication
512     // Use ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size and ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size (negotiated during handshake)
513     memcpy(ctx->client_challenge_nonce, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
514     log_info("Password authentication successful");
515   } else {
516     // Ed25519 signature auth (payload format: signature + client_nonce + gpg_key_id_len + gpg_key_id)
517     size_t expected_min_signature_size =
518         ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size + 1; // +1 for gpg_key_id_len byte
519     size_t expected_password_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
520     if (!payload) {
521       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Payload is NULL in signature authentication");
522       return ERROR_CRYPTO;
523     }
524     if (payload_len >= expected_min_signature_size) {
525       // Signature + client nonce + optional GPG key ID - VERIFY the signature on the challenge nonce
526       const uint8_t *signature = payload;
527       const uint8_t *client_nonce = payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size;
528 
529       // Extract GPG key ID if present
530       size_t gpg_offset = ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
531       uint8_t gpg_key_id_len = payload[gpg_offset];
532       const char *client_gpg_key_id = NULL;
533       char gpg_key_id_buffer[41] = {0};
534 
535       if (gpg_key_id_len > 0 && gpg_key_id_len <= 40) {
536         // Validate packet has enough bytes for GPG key ID
537         if (payload_len >= gpg_offset + 1 + gpg_key_id_len) {
538           memcpy(gpg_key_id_buffer, payload + gpg_offset + 1, gpg_key_id_len);
539           gpg_key_id_buffer[gpg_key_id_len] = '\0';
540           client_gpg_key_id = gpg_key_id_buffer;
541           log_debug("Extracted client GPG key ID from AUTH_RESPONSE: %s", client_gpg_key_id);
542         }
543       }
544 
545       // Actually verify the Ed25519/GPG signature on the challenge nonce
546       // This was missing, allowing authentication bypass
547       if (ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified) {
548         // Use unified verification function that handles both Ed25519 and GPG keys
549         // This matches the KEY_EXCHANGE verification path
550         log_debug("Verifying %s signature on challenge nonce",
551                   ctx->client_ed25519_key.type == KEY_TYPE_GPG ? "GPG" : "Ed25519");
552 
553         asciichat_error_t verify_result = ed25519_verify_signature(
554             ctx->client_ed25519_key.key, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size, signature,
555             client_gpg_key_id // GPG key ID for fallback verification
556         );
557 
558         if (verify_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
559           auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
560           failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED;
561           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "%s signature verification failed on challenge nonce",
562                     ctx->client_ed25519_key.type == KEY_TYPE_GPG ? "GPG" : "Ed25519");
563           packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
564           return ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH;
565         }
566         log_debug("%s signature on challenge nonce verified successfully",
567                   ctx->client_ed25519_key.type == KEY_TYPE_GPG ? "GPG" : "Ed25519");
568       }
569 
570       memcpy(ctx->client_challenge_nonce, client_nonce, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
571     } else if (payload_len == expected_password_size) {
572       // Just client nonce (legacy or password-only mode without password enabled)
573       // Use ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size and ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size (negotiated during handshake)
574       memcpy(ctx->client_challenge_nonce, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
575     } else {
576       // Validate packet size using session parameters
577       asciichat_error_t validation_result =
578           crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE, payload_len);
579       if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
580         return validation_result;
581       }
582     }
583   }
584 
585   // Verify client key if required (whitelist)
586   if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
587     if (!ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified) {
588       // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
589       auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
590 
591       // Check if client provided a key but it wasn't in whitelist
592       if (ctx->client_sent_identity) {
593         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Client key authentication failed - your key is not in the server's whitelist");
594         failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED;
595       } else {
596         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Client key authentication failed - client did not provide a key");
597         failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED;
598       }
599 
600       if (ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password) {
601         // Password was verified, but key was not
602         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Note: Password was correct, but client key is required");
603       }
604       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
605       return ERROR_NETWORK;
606     }
607     log_info("Client key authentication successful (whitelist verified)");
608     if (strlen(ctx->client_ed25519_key.comment) > 0) {
609       log_info("Authenticated client: %s", ctx->client_ed25519_key.comment);
610     }
611   }
612 
613   // Send SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE with server's HMAC for mutual authentication
614   // Bind to DH shared_secret to prevent MITM (even if attacker knows password)
615   // Allocate buffer using negotiated hmac_size (should match AUTH_HMAC_SIZE)
616   uint8_t server_hmac[HMAC_SHA256_SIZE]; // Maximum size, actual size is ctx->hmac_size
617   crypto_result_t crypto_result =
618       crypto_compute_auth_response(&ctx->crypto_ctx, ctx->client_challenge_nonce, server_hmac);
619   if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
620     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to compute server HMAC for mutual authentication: %s",
621               crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
622     return ERROR_NETWORK;
623   }
624 
625   log_debug("Sending SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE packet with server HMAC (%u bytes) "
626             "for mutual authentication",
627             ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size);
628   result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP, server_hmac,
629                                      ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size, 0);
630   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
631     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE packet");
632     return ERROR_NETWORK;
633   }
634 
635   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
636   log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully (mutual authentication)");
637 
638   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
639 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED, AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED,
    AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT, crypto_context_t::auth_nonce,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_challenge_nonce,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key_verified,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_sent_identity,
    public_key_t::comment, crypto_compute_auth_response(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), crypto_verify_auth_response(),
    ed25519_verify_signature(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, crypto_context_t::has_password,
    HMAC_SHA256_SIZE, crypto_context_t::hmac_size,
    public_key_t::key, crypto_context_t::key_exchange_complete,
    KEY_TYPE_GPG, log_debug, log_info,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP,
    auth_failure_packet_t::reason_flags,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_context_t::signature_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and public_key_t::type.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    session_server_like_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t *
  transport)


Server: Send CRYPTO_PARAMETERS and set handshake context
  sizes.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context


transport ACIP transport to send on


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Must be called before crypto_handshake_server_start().
    Builds crypto parameters based on server state (identity key presence),
    sends them to the client, and updates context sizes to match.




TCP server path calls this indirectly via its own negotiation in
    src/server/crypto.c (which customizes parameters based on client
    capabilities). WebSocket and discovery-service paths call this directly.
    <X25519 key exchange (Curve25519)




<Ed25519 authentication (Edwards-curve signatures)




<No authentication (plaintext mode)




<XSalsa20-Poly1305 authenticated encryption


Definition at line 20 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


20                                                                                                                         {
21   if (!ctx || !transport) {
22     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, transport=%p", (void *)ctx, (void *)transport);
23   }
24 
25   bool has_identity_key = (ctx->server_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
26 
27   crypto_parameters_packet_t params;
28   memset(&params, 0, sizeof(params));
29 
30   params.selected_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
31   params.selected_auth = has_identity_key ? AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 : AUTH_ALGO_NONE;
32   params.selected_cipher = CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305;
33   params.verification_enabled = ctx->require_client_auth ? 1 : 0;
34   params.kex_public_key_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size;
35   params.shared_secret_size = CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE;
36   params.nonce_size = XSALSA20_NONCE_SIZE;
37   params.mac_size = POLY1305_MAC_SIZE;
38   params.hmac_size = 32; // SHA256 output size
39 
40   if (has_identity_key) {
41     params.auth_public_key_size = ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE;
42     params.signature_size = ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;
43   }
44 
45   // Set context sizes to match what we send (host byte order)
46   crypto_handshake_set_parameters(ctx, &params);
47 
48   // Convert to network byte order for the wire
49   params.kex_public_key_size = htons(params.kex_public_key_size);
50   params.auth_public_key_size = htons(params.auth_public_key_size);
51   params.signature_size = htons(params.signature_size);
52   params.shared_secret_size = htons(params.shared_secret_size);
53 
54   int result =
55       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS, (uint8_t *)&params, sizeof(params), 0);
56   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
57     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send CRYPTO_PARAMETERS packet");
58   }
59 
60   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE] CRYPTO_PARAMETERS sent: auth=%s, cipher=XSALSA20_POLY1305",
61             has_identity_key ? "ED25519" : "NONE");
62   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
63 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, AUTH_ALGO_ED25519,
    AUTH_ALGO_NONE,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_set_parameters(), CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE,
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::hmac_size, KEX_ALGO_X25519,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size,
    KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::mac_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::nonce_size,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS,
    POLY1305_MAC_SIZE, crypto_context_t::public_key_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_cipher,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_kex,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size,
    private_key_t::type,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::verification_enabled, and
    XSALSA20_NONCE_SIZE.


Referenced by session_server_like_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_start
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t *
  transport)


Server: Start crypto handshake by sending public key.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT state)


transport ACIP transport to send on


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Server initiates handshake by sending KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet.
    Supports both simple and authenticated formats based on server identity
  key.




Note


Packet formats:

    
  
  • Simple: [ephemeral_key:public_key_size] (when server has no identity
    key)
    • 
  
  • Authenticated:
      [ephemeral_key:public_key_size][identity_key:auth_public_key_size][signature:signature_size]
      (when server has Ed25519 identity key)
    • 



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE




Definition at line 66 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


66                                                                                                               {
67   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] ===== ENTRY: crypto_handshake_server_start called =====");
68   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] ctx=%p, transport=%p", (void *)ctx, (void *)transport);
69 
70   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT) {
71     log_error("[HANDSHAKE_START] FAILED: Invalid state - ctx=%p, state=%d", (void *)ctx, ctx ? (int)ctx->state : -1);
72     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", (void *)ctx, ctx ? (int)ctx->state : -1);
73   }
74   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] State check OK: state=%d (CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT)", ctx->state);
75 
76   int result;
77 
78   // Caller is responsible for sending CRYPTO_PARAMETERS and calling
79   // crypto_handshake_set_parameters() before this function. This function
80   // only handles KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT.
81 
82   // Calculate packet size based on negotiated crypto parameters
83   size_t expected_packet_size =
84       ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size;
85 
86   log_debug("SERVER_KEY_EXCHANGE: kex_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u, expected_size=%zu",
87             ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size,
88             expected_packet_size);
89 
90   // Check if we have an identity key to send authenticated packet
91   if (ctx->server_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
92     // Extended packet format:
93     // [ephemeral_key:kex_size][identity_key:auth_size][signature:sig_size]
94     uint8_t *extended_packet;
95     extended_packet = SAFE_MALLOC(expected_packet_size, uint8_t *);
96     if (!extended_packet) {
97       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for extended packet");
98     }
99 
100     // Copy ephemeral public key
101     memcpy(extended_packet, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
102 
103     // Copy identity public key
104     memcpy(extended_packet + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->server_private_key.public_key,
105            ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size);
106 
107     // DEBUG: Print identity key being sent
108     char hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
109     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
110       safe_snprintf(hex + i * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->server_private_key.public_key[i]);
111     }
112     hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32 - 1] = '\0';
113 
114     // Sign the ephemeral key with our identity key
115     log_debug("Signing ephemeral key with server identity key");
116 
117     // Log key details in debug mode
118     char hex_ephemeral[65], hex_identity[65];
119     for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
120       safe_snprintf(hex_ephemeral + i * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key[i]);
121       safe_snprintf(hex_identity + i * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->server_private_key.public_key[i]);
122     }
123     hex_ephemeral[64] = hex_identity[64] = '\0';
124     log_debug("SERVER: Ephemeral key (32 bytes): %s", hex_ephemeral);
125     log_debug("SERVER: Identity public key: %s", hex_identity);
126 
127     if (ed25519_sign_message(&ctx->server_private_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
128                              extended_packet + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size +
129                                  ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size) != 0) {
130       SAFE_FREE(extended_packet);
131       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to sign ephemeral key with identity key");
132     }
133 
134     log_debug("Sending authenticated KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes: ephemeral + "
135               "identity + signature)",
136               expected_packet_size);
137     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] Calling packet_send_via_transport(type=%d, payload_len=%zu, 0)...",
138               PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, expected_packet_size);
139     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, extended_packet,
140                                        expected_packet_size, 0);
141     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] packet_send_via_transport returned %d", result);
142     SAFE_FREE(extended_packet);
143   } else {
144     // No identity key - send just the ephemeral key
145     log_debug("Sending simple KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes: ephemeral key only)", ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
146     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] Calling packet_send_via_transport(type=%d, payload_len=%zu, 0)...",
147               PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
148     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key,
149                                        ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, 0);
150     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] packet_send_via_transport returned %d", result);
151   }
152 
153   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
154     log_error("[HANDSHAKE_START] FAILED: packet_send returned %d", result);
155     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet");
156   }
157   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] ===== SUCCESS: KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT sent =====");
158 
159   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE;
160 
161   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
162 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_context_t::auth_public_key_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,
    ed25519_sign_message(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    HEX_STRING_SIZE_32, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    log_error, packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT,
    crypto_context_t::public_key, private_key_t::public_key,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_context_t::signature_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and
  private_key_t::type.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    session_server_like_handshake().

    ascii-chat
